                },
                "frequency": "yearly",
                "interval": 1,
                "repeatWeekday": true,
                "preferredTechs": []
                "preferredVendor": null,
                "estimatedPrice": null,
                "duration": 0,
                "created": 1370266450,
                "updated": 1371456236
            }
        ]
    }
}

Retrieve one service template

GET /servicetemplate/222

200
{
    "data": {
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/servicetemplate/222",
        "id": 222,
        "name": "Quarterly Inspection",
        "description": "Inspect all the quarters, and make sure they are shiny",
        "serviceLine": {
            "id": 4,
            "name": "Fire Suppression",
            "trade": "Fire Protection",
            "abbr": "SUPP",
            "icon": "https://api.servicetrade.com/image/icons/service_lines/32/SUPP.png"
        },
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "interval": 3,
        "repeatWeekday": false,
        "preferredTechs": [],
        "preferredVendor": null,
        "estimatedPrice": 12.34,
        "duration": 3600,
        "created": 1370266418,
        "updated": 1370544276
    }
}

Create a template

POST /servicetemplate
{"name": "Quarterly Inspection", "description": "Inspect all the quarters, and make sure they are shiny", "serviceLineId": 4, "frequency": "monthly", "interval": 3}

200
{
    "data": {
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/servicetemplate/222",
        "id": 222,
        "name": "Quarterly Inspection",
        "description": "Inspect all the quarters, and make sure they are shiny",
        "serviceLine": {
            "id": 4,
            "name": "Fire Suppression",
            "trade": "Fire Protection",
            "abbr": "SUPP",
            "icon": "https://api.servicetrade.com/image/icons/service_lines/32/SUPP.png"
        },
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "interval": 3,
        "repeatWeekday": false,
        "preferredTechs": [],
        "preferredVendor": null,
        "estimatedPrice": null,
        "duration": null,
        "created": 1370266418,
        "updated": 1370266418
    }
}

Return to Top

Tag

The tag resource is used to retrieve lists of tags or detailed information about a single tag. It can also be used to add a tag to an entity or to remove it.

  • Resource URL: /tag/[tagId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • Authentication Required: yes

The following entities support tags:

  • Asset
  • Company
  • Contract
  • Deficiency
  • Invoice
  • Job
  • Location
  • Quote

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /tag 200
GET /tag/123 200
GET /tag/123 404 tag 123 not found
POST /tag 200
POST /tag 403 no permission
POST /tag 404 tried to create a tag for an object that wasn't found
POST /tag 405 not allowed
POST /tag/123/merge 200 merge a tag into another
POST /tag/123/merge 403 no permission
POST /tag/123/merge 400 invalid params
PUT /tag/123 200 rename tag
PUT /tag/123 409 new tag name already in use
PUT /tag/123 403 no permission
DELETE /tag 204 tag deleted
DELETE /tag 403 no permission
DELETE /tag 404 attempting to delete from an entity that wasn't found
DELETE /tag 405 not allowed

Query Params

Name Type Required Value
entityId integer No tags attached to this entity id only (if set, must also provide entityType)
entityType entity type No tags attached to this entity type only (if set, must also provide entityId)
name string No return tags with names containing this text
exactName string No return tags that match this text exactly
counts boolean No if true, response includes a count of how many entities this tag is assigned to, grouped by entity type

POST Parameters

Name Type Required Value
entityId integer No the id of the entity to apply the tag to (if set, you must also provide entityType and either id or name)
entityType integer No the type of the entity to apply the tag to (if set, you must also provide entityId and either id or name)
id integer No the id of the tag you wish to apply, must also provide entityId and entityType
name string No the name of the tag you wish to create - if one by that name already exists it will be returned without creating a duplicate

Merge Parameters

The merge endpoint replaces this tag (the loser) with another (the winner) on all entities that are assigned the losing tag. The loser is then deleted.

Name Type Required Value
replacementId integer Yes the id of the tag you wish to merge this tag into, the "winner"

PUT Parameters

Name Type Required Value
name string Yes the new name for this tag - must not already exist

DELETE Parameters

Name Type Required Value
entityId integer No the id of the entity to remove the tag from, Required unless deleting tag from ALL entities
entityType integer No the type of the entity to remove the tag from, Required unless deleting tag from ALL entities
id integer No the id of the tag you wish to remove, Required if no name is provided
name string No the name of the tag you wish to remove, Required if no id is provided

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
tags array (Tag) list of tag objects

Get all tags

    GET /api/tag?counts=1
     200
    {
        "data": {
            "totalPages": 1,
            "page": 1,
            "tags": [
                {
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/1",
                    "id": 1,
                    "name": "Mall",
                    "counts": [
                        {
                            "entityType": 5,
                            "entityName": "Company",
                            "count": 3
                        }
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/12",
                    "id": 12,
                    "name": "CallManagerFirst"
                }
            ]
        }
    }

Get a single tag

    GET /api/tag/1
     200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/1",
            "id": 1,
            "name": "Mall"
        }
    }

Create a new tag

    POST /api/tag?name=MyNewTag
     200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/765",
            "id": 765,
            "name": "MyNewTag"
        }
    }

Attach tag to entity using name, will create tag if it doesn't already exist

    POST /api/tag?name=MyNewTag&entityId=1&entityType=11
     200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/765",
            "id": 765,
            "name": "MyNewTag"
        }
    }

Attach tag to entity using id

    POST /api/tag/765?entityId=1&entityType=11
     200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/765",
            "id": 765,
            "name": "MyNewTag"
        }
    }

Rename an existing tag

    PUT /api/tag/756
    {"name": "TheNewTagName"}

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/765",
            "id": 765,
            "name": "TheNewTagName"
        }
    }

Merge a tag into another

    POST /api/tag/756/merge
    {"replacementId": 987}

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tag/765",
            "id": 987,
            "name": "MyNewFavoriteTag"
        }
    }

Remove tag from entity using name

    DELETE /api/tag?name=MyNewTag&entityId=1&entityType=11
     200

Remove tag from entity using id

    DELETE /api/tag/765?entityId=1&entityType=11
     200

Return to Top

Task

The task resource is used to retrieve the list of tasks for different entities or detailed information about a single task.

  • Resource URL: /task/[taskId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /task 405 unconditional index not allowed, provide taskListId or referenceNumber
GET /task 403 no permission
GET /task/123 200
GET /task/123 404 task not found
GET /task/123 403 no permission
POST /task 200 task created
POST /task 403 no permission
POST /task 400 invalid post data
PUT /task/123 200
PUT /task/123 404 task not found
PUT /task/123 403 no permission
PUT /task/123 400 invalid PUT data
DELETE /task/123 405 not allowed

Query Params

Name Type Value
taskListId integer returns all tasks for tasklist taskListId
referenceNumber string returns all tasks matching referenceNumber
status string returns all tasks matching the status, one of 'active', 'inactive'
assetId integer returns all tasks for tasklist asset_id

Note: taskListId or referenceNumber are required for an index request

Parameters for POST/PUT

Name Type Required on POST Allowed on PUT? Value
taskListId integer yes no id for the tasklist the task belongs to. this can be a tasklist of type tasklist or of type template
sectionId integer no yes id for the section type tasklist the task belongs to
name string yes yes the descriptive text for this task
inputType string yes yes the type of the input expected, one of ('checkbox', 'date', 'text', 'multiple_choice', 'number')
referenceNumber string no no the reference number for this task used to correlate with its task instances and other versions of the same task. may provide one on POST or accept one assigned by default.
orderIndex integer no yes indicates order of display with other tasks/sections on the tasklist
status string no yes one of ('active', 'inactive')
duration integer no yes estimated duration for the task in minutes, or null for no estimate
frequencyCategory string yes yes one of ('quarterly', 'monthly', 'semi-annually', 'annually', 'multi-year')
events array no yes array of YYYY-MM-DD strings of month start dates. Required to be the first of the month. Duplicate events of the same month but different year (e.g., ["2024-01-01", "2023-01-01"]) are not allowed unless the task's event frequency is non-repeating.
eventFrequency string no yes event frequency, one of ('yearly', 'nonrepeating')
eventInterval integer no yes interval of the events. all nonrepeating events have a null interval (it will be null by default, non-null values will be rejected). yearly intervals must be an integer of 1 or greater (it will be 1 by default or if null is provided). A yearly event with an interval of 3 occurs every 3 years for example.
eventWindow string no yes one of ('month'). currently all task events have a window of one calendar month
choiceType string no yes one of ('choose_many', 'choose_one') for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's
choices string[] no yes array of strings for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's. Cannot contain empty strings.

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
id integer item id
uri string resource identifier for the given task
tasklist Task List the 'template' or 'tasklist' type tasklist to which this task belongs
section Task List the 'section' type tasklist to which this task belongs if any
name string the descriptive text for this task
status string status of the task, one of ('active', 'inactive')
inputType string the type of the input expected, one of ('checkbox', 'date', 'text', 'multiple_choice', 'number')
referenceNumber string the reference number for this task used to correlate with its task instances and other versions of the same task
orderIndex integer indicates order of display with other tasks/sections on the tasklist
frequencyCategory string one of ('quarterly', 'monthly', 'semi-annually', 'annually', 'multi-year')
duration integer estimated duration for the task in minutes, or null for no estimate
events array array of YYYY-MM-DD strings of month start dates.
eventFrequency string event frequency, one of ('yearly', 'nonrepeating')
eventInterval integer interval of the events
eventWindow string one of ('month'). currently all task events have a window of one calendar month
choices string[] array of strings for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's
choiceType json one of ('choose_many', 'choose_one') for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's
asset Asset connected asset of tasklist (only for /task endpoint)

Get all tasks for a assetId

GET /task?taskListId=18475309

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "tasks": [
            {
                "id": 1045358147108865,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045358147108865",
                "referenceNumber": "1045358147108865",
                "name": "very carefully check voltage",
                "status": "active",
                "orderIndex": 2,
                "taskList": {
                    "id": 8675309,
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/8675309",
                    "name": "Service Agreement",
                    "status": "active",
                    "type": "task_list"
                },
                "section": null,
                "inputType": "text",
                "frequencyCategory": "monthly",
                "duration": 60,
                "events": [
                    "2023-03-01",
                    "2023-09-01"
                ],
                "eventFrequency": "yearly",
                "eventInterval": 1,
                "eventWindow": "month",
                "choiceType": null,
                "choices": null,
                "asset": {
                    "id": 18475309,
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/asset/18475309",
                    "serviceLine": null,
                    "legacyId": 44222,
                    "type": "location",
                    "status": "active",
                    "display": "Location",
                    "name": "Building",
                    "properties": {},
                    "parent": null,
                    "created": 1460130947,
                    "updated": 1460130947,
                    "orderIndex": null,
                }
            },
            {
                "id": 1045367025664001,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045367025664001",
                "referenceNumber": "1045367025664001",
                "name": "copy status code",
                "status": "active",
                "orderIndex": 0,
                "taskList": {
                    "id": 8675309,
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/8675309",
                    "name": "Service Agreement",
                    "status": "active",
                    "type": "task_list"
                },
                "section": null,
                "inputType": "text",
                "frequencyCategory": "monthly",
                "duration": null,
                "events": [
                    "2023-01-01",
                ],
                "eventFrequency": "nonrepeating",
                "eventInterval": null,
                "eventWindow": "month",

                "choices": null,
                "asset": {
                    "id": 18475309,
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/asset/18475309",
                    "serviceLine": null,
                    "legacyId": 44222,
                    "type": "location",
                    "status": "active",
                    "display": "Location",
                    "name": "Building",
                    "properties": {},
                    "parent": null,
                    "created": 1460130947,
                    "updated": 1460130947,
                    "orderIndex": null,
                }
            }
        ]
    }
}

Get all tasks for a reference number

GET /task?referenceNumber=1045358147108865

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "tasks": [
            {
                "id": 1045358147108865,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045358147108865",
                "referenceNumber": "1045358147108865",
                "name": "very carefully check voltage",
                "status": "active",
                "orderIndex": 2,
                "taskList": {
                    "id": 8675309,
                    "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/8675309",

                },

        ]

Retrieve a task by id

GET /task/1045358147108865

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 1045358147108865,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045358147108865",

        "name": "very carefully check voltage",

Create a new task (repeating)

POST /task
{"name":"check voltage", "taskListId":1001, "frequencyCategory":"monthly", "inputType":"multiple_choice", "events": ["2023-03-01", "2023-09-01"], "eventFrequency": "yearly", "interval": 1, "choiceType": "choose_one", "choices": [] }

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 1065181999005697,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1065181999005697",
        "referenceNumber": "1065181999005697",
        "name": "check voltage",
        "status": "active",
        "orderIndex": 0,
        "taskList": {
            "id": 1001,
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/1001",
            "name": "my tasklist",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "task_list"
        },
        "section": null,
        "inputType": "multiple_choice",
        "frequencyCategory": "monthly",
        "duration": null,
        "events": [
            "2023-03-01",
            "2023-09-01"
        ],
        "eventFrequency": "yearly",
        "eventInterval": 1,
        "eventWindow": "month",
        "choiceType": "choose_one",
        "choices": []
    }
}

Create a new task (non-repeating)

POST /task
{"name":"check voltage", "taskListId":1001, "frequencyCategory":"monthly", "inputType":"text", "events": ["2023-03-01", "2023-09-01"], "eventFrequency": "nonrepeating"}

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 1065181999005697,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1065181999005697",
        "referenceNumber": "1065181999005697",
        "name": "check voltage",
        "status": "active",
        "orderIndex": 0,
        "taskList": {
            "id": 1001,
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/1001",
            "name": "my tasklist",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "task_list"
        },
        "section": null,
        "inputType": "text",
        "frequencyCategory": "monthly",
        "duration": null,
        "events": [
            "2023-03-01",
            "2023-09-01"
        ],
        "eventFrequency": "nonrepeating",
        "eventInterval": null,
        "eventWindow": "month",
        "choiceType": null,
        "choices": null
    }
}

Modify an existing task

PUT /task/1045358147108865
{"name":"very carefully check voltage", "orderIndex":2, "frequencyCategory":"quarterly", "duration":60}

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 1045358147108865,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045358147108865",
        "referenceNumber": "1045358147108865",
        "name": "very carefully check voltage",
        "status": "active",
        "orderIndex": 2,
        "taskList": {
            "id": 8675309,
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/8675309",
            "name": "Service Agreement",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "task_list"
        },
        "section": null,
        "inputType": "text",
        "frequencyCategory": "quarterly",
        "duration": 60,
        "events": [
            "2023-03-01",
            "2023-09-01"
        ],
        "eventFrequency": "yearly",
        "eventInterval": 1,
        "eventWindow": "month",
        "choiceType": null,
        "choices": null
    }
}

Modify an existing task by changing its schedule

PUT /task/1045358147108865
{"events": ['2023-06-01', '2023-08-01']}

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 1045358147108865,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1045358147108865",
        "referenceNumber": "1045358147108865",
        "name": "very carefully check voltage",
        "status": "active",
        "orderIndex": 2,
        "taskList": {
            "id": 8675309,
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/8675309",
            "name": "Service Agreement",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "task_list"
        },
        "section": null,
        "inputType": "text",
        "frequencyCategory": "quarterly",
        "duration": 60,
        "events": [
            "2023-06-01",
            "2023-08-01"
        ],
        "eventFrequency": "yearly",
        "eventInterval": 1,
        "eventWindow": "month",
        "choiceType": null,
        "choices": null
    }
}

Return to Top

Taskinstance

The task instances source is used to retrieve the list of task instances for different entities or detailed information about a single task instance.

  • Resource URL: /taskinstance/[taskinstanceId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, PUT
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /taskinstance 405 unconditional index not allowed, provide taskListId, taskId, referenceNumber or serviceRequestId
GET /taskinstance 403 no permission
GET /taskinstance/123 200
GET /taskinstance/123 404 task instance not found
GET /taskinstance/123 403 no permission
POST /taskinstance 405 not allowed
PUT /taskinstance/123 200
PUT /taskinstance/123 404 task instance not found
PUT /taskinstance/123 403 no permission
PUT /taskinstance/123 400 invalid PUT data
DELETE /taskinstance/123 405 not allowed

Query Params

Name Type Value
taskListId integer returns all task instances for task list taskListId
taskId integer returns all task instances for task taskId
referenceNumber string returns all task instances matching referenceNumber
serviceRequestId integer returns all task instances for service request serviceRequestId
jobId integer returns all task instances for job jobId
assetId integer returns all task instances for tasklist asset assetId
status string returns all task instances of this status, one of: ["open", "completed", "ignored"]

Note: taskListId, taskId, referenceNumber or serviceRequestId are required for an index request

PUT Parameters

Name Type Required Value
serviceRequestId integer no id of the service request the task instance belongs to
status string no status of the task instance, used in displaying state of tasks in mobile app, one of: ["open", "completed", "ignored"]. If status is not provided, but response is, then status will automatically set to "completed".
orderIndex integer no indicates order of display with other tasks/sections on the tasklist
response string no response
responseDate integer no unix timestamp of response (in case if response is sent without this value then it is set to current timestamp)
responseUserId integer no id of the user who responded (in case if response is sent without user then responseUserId is set to current user)

Special PUT Parameter: response

response is always an array of strings, but the exact format will vary by input type

Name For inputType Type Required Value
response text array of a string no one element array of the response text e.g. ['this is my response']
response date array of a string no one element array of the response date in YY-MM-DD format e.g. ['2023-07-04']
response number array of a string or number no one element array of the response number e.g. ['3.14159']
response checkbox array of a string or boolean no one element array of the response boolean e.g. ['1'], ['0'], ['true'], ['false'], [true], or [false]
response multiple_choice (choiceType = 'choose_one') array of strings no empty array or one element array of the selection text e.g. ['default']
response multiple_choice (choiceType = 'choose_many') array of strings no empty or N element array of the selection text(s) e.g. ['default', 'premium']

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
id integer item id
uri string resource identifier for the given task instance
task Task the 'template' or 'task' type task to which this task instance belongs
serviceRequest ServiceRequest the service request that uses this task instance
referenceNumber integer the reference number, used to identify our task instances and other earlier and later versions of this task
sectionName string the name of the section this task instance belongs to or null if none
name string the name of the task, when created, this task instance belongs to
status string status of the task instance, used in displaying state of tasks in mobile app, one of: ["open", "completed", "ignored"]. defaults to "completed" for task instances with a response unless explicitly overridden.
orderIndex integer indicates order of display with other task instances on the task
response array array of responses
respondedOn integer UNIX timestamp when did the response happen
responseUser User a user who responded
frequencyCategory string one of ('quarterly', 'monthly', 'semi-annually', 'annually', 'multi-year')
duration integer estimated duration for the task instance in minutes, or null for no estimate
forPeriodStarting string 'YYYY-MM-DD' the starting date as a number string signifying the start of the window that this instance was created for
forPeriodEnding string 'YYYY-MM-DD' the ending date as a number string signifying the end of the window that this instance was created for
inputType string the type of the input expected, one of ('checkbox', 'date', 'text', 'multiple_choice', 'number')
choices string[] array of strings for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's
choiceType string one of ('choose_many', 'choose_one') for inputType 'multiple_choice' or null for other inputType's
sectionOrderIndex integer indicates order of display with other task instances on the section
asset Asset connected asset of the task list that this task instance was produced by
serviceRequestAsset Asset connected asset of the service request this task instance is assigned to (if asset is a sub asset then this will typically be its ancestor toplevel asset)

Retrieve all task instances with one of the above required parameters

    GET /taskinstance?assetId=35054514708

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "totalPages": 1,
            "page": 1,

Retrieve detail information for a task instances which does not exist

    GET /taskinstance/987

    404

Post a task instances not allowed

    POST /taskinstance

    405

Modify a task instances to set 'serviceRequestId', 'orderIndex', 'response', 'responseDate', 'responseUserId', 'status'

 NOTE: If response is sent and responseDate is not sent then responseDate will be set to current datetime.
 Similarly if response is sent and responseUserId is not sent then responseUserId will be set to current user.


    PUT /taskinstance/987
    {
        "serviceRequestId": 1054990263353408,
        "status": "completed",
        "orderIndex": 10,
        "response": ["some response"],
        "responseDate": 1687305600,
        "responseUserId": 1054989092864064,
    }

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "id": 1054989095534656,
            "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taskinstace/1054989095534656",
            "name": "Task Name",
            "status": "completed",
            "task": {
                "id": 1054989089505344,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/task/1054989089505344",
                "referenceNumber": "597540938",
                "name": "Check Voltage",
                "status": "active",
                "orderIndex": 12
            },
            "serviceRequest": {
                "id": 1054990263353408,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/servicerequest/1054990263353408",
                "description": "SR 559544390"
            },
            "referenceNumber": "597540938",
            "sectionName": "Some section",
            "orderIndex": 56,
            "response": ["some response"],
            "respondedOn": 1687305600,
            "responseUser": {
                "id": 1054989092864064,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/user/1054989092864064",
                "name": "Arthur Dent",
                "status": "active",
                "email": "test423185762@example.com",
                "avatar": {
                    "small": "https://servicetrade.com/image/avatar-small.png",
                    "medium": "https://servicetrade.com/image/avatar-medium.png",
                    "large": "https://servicetrade.com/image/avatar-large.png"
                },
                "isTech": false,
                "isHelper": false
            },
            "frequencyCategory": "monthly",
            "duration": null,
            "forPeriodStarting": "2023-06-11",
            "forPeriodEnding": "2023-06-30",
            "choiceType": "choose_one",
            "choices": [],
            "sectionOrderIndex": 1,
            "asset": {
                "id": 1103604776730625,
                "uri": "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/asset/1103604776730625",
                "type": "alarm",
                "name": "Alarm System - Alarmo 3000"
            },
            "serviceRequestAsset": {
                "id": 1103604776730625,
                "uri": "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/asset/1103604776730625",
                "type": "alarm",
                "name": "Alarm System - Alarmo 3000"
            }
        }
    }

Delete a task instances not allowed

    DELETE /taskinstance/987

    405

Return to Top

Tasklist

The task list source is used to retrieve the list of task lists for different entities or detailed information about a single task list.

  • Resource URL: /tasklist/[tasklistId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, PUT, POST
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /tasklist 405 unconditional index not allowed, provide one of: type, status, assetDefinitionId, assetId, startsOn, endsOn.
GET /tasklist/123 404 task list not found
GET /tasklist/123 403 no permission
GET /tasklist/123 200 task list found
POST /tasklist 400 invalid POST data
POST /tasklist 200 task list created
POST /tasklist/123/copy 200
POST /tasklist/123/copy 400 illegal request
POST /tasklist/123/copy 403 insufficient permissions to perform action
POST /tasklist/123/copy 404 tasklist 123 not found
POST /tasklist/123/copy 409 tasklist 123 is the wrong type or status to perform this action on
POST /tasklist/123/updateall 200
POST /tasklist/123/updateall 400 illegal request
POST /tasklist/123/updateall 403 insufficient permissions to perform action
POST /tasklist/123/updateall 404 tasklist 123 not found
PUT /tasklist/123 404 task list not found
PUT /tasklist/123 403 no permission
PUT /tasklist/123 400 invalid PUT data
PUT /tasklist/123 200 task list updated
DELETE /tasklist/123 405 not allowed

Query Params

Note: At least one of these parameters must be set.

Name Type Valid For Value
type string all types returns all task lists of this type
status string all types returns all task lists of this status
assetDefinitionIds integer templates returns all task lists associated with this asset definition
assetDefinitionIdsIntersect integer templates returns all task lists associated with all of these asset definitions
assetId integer tasklists returns all task lists assigned to this asset id
assetIds array(integer) tasklists returns all task lists assigned to any of these asset ids
startsOnBefore string templates and tasklists returns all task lists with a startOn before this date, format should be YYYY-MM-DD
startsOnAfter string templates and tasklists returns all task lists with a startOn after this date, format should be YYYY-MM-DD
endsOnBefore string templates and tasklists returns all task lists with a endsOn before this date, format should be YYYY-MM-DD
endsOnAfter string templates and tasklists returns all task lists with a endsOn after this date, format should be YYYY-MM-DD
searchName string templates returns all task lists which a name containing this substring
sort string all types instructions for sorting the results, one of "created" or "usage". "usage" is only meaningful for template type task lists - other types will be sorted with a value of 0. "usage" is a count of how many currently active task lists assigned to an asset were created from a particular template.
sortOrder string all types order of sort, either ASC or DESC (ASC is the default)

POST Params

Name Type For type "task_list" For type "template" For type "section" Value
type string required required required Type of this task list. One of: "section", "task_list", "template".
status string optional optional optional Status of this section/tasklist/template. One of: "active", "inactive". Defaults to "active".
name string required required required Name of this section/template/tasklist.
scheduleType string optional optional null or omitted The scheduling cadence type of this tasklist or template. Only valid value is "monthly", and defaults to "monthly".
parentId integer null or omitted null or omitted required The parent tasklist (of type "task_list" or "template") that this section belongs to.
templateId integer optional null or omitted null or omitted The template that this task list was created from. If provided alongside assetId, must be an template that covers the asset definition of the provided asset.
orderIndex integer null or omitted null or omitted optional The zero-indexed order of this section on its parent tasklist (e.g., 0 - this section is the first item on its parent tasklist).
startsOn string optional optional null or omitted The date at which this tasklist comes into effect, in format "YYYY-MM-DD". (only January 1 dates are allowed for template type task lists)
endsOn string optional optional null or omitted The date at which this tasklist stops being in effect, in format "YYYY-MM-DD".
assetDefinitionIds array(integer) null or omitted optional null or omitted The asset definition type that this template can be applied to.
assetId integer optional null or omitted null or omitted The asset that this task list is associated with. If provided alongside templateId, must be an asset that is associated with the asset definitions for that template.

POST Parameters for /api/tasklist/:id/updateall

Name Type Required Value
assetId integer required linked asset id
tasks array(object) required Array of objects with schedule details: ('events', 'frequency','interval')
tasks.events array(string) required Array of dates (['2023-01-01','2023-02-01'])
tasks.eventsFrequency string required event frequency, one of ('yearly', 'nonrepeating')
tasks.frequencyCategory string required one of ('quarterly', 'monthly', 'semi-annually', 'annually', 'multi-year')
tasks.interval null or integer required Interval of repeat
tasks.referenceNumber integer required Reference number of task
tasks.estimatedDuration integer or null required estimated duration for the task in minutes, or null for no estimate
tasks.name string optional the descriptive text for this task

POST Parameters for /api/tasklist/:id/copy

Name Type Required Value
targetType string required one of ('task_list', 'template'), determines the type of the copy
assetDefinitionIds array(integer) optional only allowed if targetType is 'template', optionally provide a list of assetDefinitionIds that the new template will apply to. defaults to be identical to the template we are copying from.
assetId integer required if targetType is 'task_list' for targetType = 'task_list' request only, the asset for which we are creating a task list
name string optional if provided, override the old name with this new name for the copy

PUT Params

Name Type For type "task_list" For type "template" For type "section" Value
status string valid parameter valid parameter valid parameter Status of this section/tasklist/template. One of: "active", "inactive".
name string valid parameter valid parameter valid parameter Name of this section/template/tasklist. If provided, cannot be empty string or null.
orderIndex integer invalid parameter invalid parameter valid parameter The zero-indexed order of this section on its parent tasklist (e.g., 0 - this section is the first item on its parent tasklist).
startsOn string valid parameter valid parameter invalid parameter The date at which this tasklist becomes active and is in effect. (only January 1 dates are allowed for template type task lists)
endsOn string valid parameter valid parameter invalid parameter The date at which this tasklist stops being active and in effect. If not provided, tasklist or template will be in effect in perpetuity until this is set.
assetDefinitionIds array(integer) invalid parameter valid parameter invalid parameter The asset definition type that this template can be applied to.
shiftMonths integer valid parameter invalid parameter invalid parameter Will shift start and end months of all active tasks associated with task list. Can only provide values between -11 and 11. Will not adjust the year if shifting pushes month past start of year.

Returned Elements

Name Type For type "task_list" For type "template" For type "section" Value
id integer returned returned returned id of section/tasklist/template
uri string returned returned returned resource identifier for the given task instance
type string returned returned returned one of: "template", "task_list", "section".
status string returned returned returned one of: "active", "inactive".
name string returned returned returned the name of this task list
scheduleType string returned returned always null null for "section", otherwise "monthly".
parent Tasklist always null always null returned null for "task_list" or "template"; for type "section" this is the tasklist (template or task_list) that the section belongs to
template Tasklist returned always null always null null for "template" and "section"; type "task_list" has this set to the template that generated it
templateState string returned always null always null one of 'up_to_date', 'out_of_date', 'modified', describing the task list's relationship with its task template
version integer or null returned returned always null null for "section", for type "template" the current version of the template. for "task_list" the version it was based on if no changes have been made. A TaskList of type "task_list" nulls this out with the first change that deviates from the template.
orderIndex integer or null always null always null returned null for "template" and "task_list"; otherwise an integer
startsOn string or null returned returned always null null for "section", otherwise YYYY-MM-DD date field of when the task list begins
endsOn string or null returned returned always null null for "section", otherwise YYYY-MM-DD date field of when the task list ends or null for ongoing
assetDefinitions array(Asset Definition) always null returned always null null for "task_list" and "section", for type "template" a list of asset definitions that this template applies to.
asset Asset returned always null always null null for "template" and "section", for type "task_list" the asset that it is assigned to
statActiveAssets integer always null returned always null null for "task_list" and "section", for type "template" the number of assets with active task lists that were generated from this template (any version)
statActiveAssetsOutdated integer always null returned always null null for "task_list" and "section", for type "template" the number of assets with active task lists that were generated from a non-current version of this template
statActiveRecurrences integer always null returned always null null for "task_list" and "section", for type "template" the number of assets with active task lists that were generated from a non-current version of this template
taskCount integer returned returned always 0 0 for "section", for type "task_list" and "template" the number of active tasks in the task list

Retrieve all task lists with one of the above required parameters

    GET /tasklist?taskListId=567

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "totalPages": 1,
            "page": 1,
            "taskLists": [
                {

            ]
        }
    }

Retrieve all task lists templates that apply to all provided asset definitions

    GET /tasklist?assetDefinitionIdsIntersect=9,12

    200
    {
        {
            data: {
                totalPages: 1,
                page: 1,
                taskLists: [
                    {
                        id: 1511354082934850,
                        uri: "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/1511354082934850",
                        name: "Fire Pump and Suppression",
                        status: "active",
                        type: "template",
                        scheduleType: "monthly",
                        parent: null,
                        template: null,
                        version: 2,
                        orderIndex: null,
                        startsOn: "2024-01-01",
                        endsOn: null,
                        asset: null,
                        statActiveAssets: 0,
                        statActiveAssetsOutdated: 0,
                        statActiveRecurrences: null,
                        taskCount: 1,
                        assetDefinitions: [
                            {
                                id: 9,
                                uri: "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/assetdefinition/9",
                                type: "suppression",
                                display: "Fire Suppression"
                                },
                            {
                            id: 12,
                                uri: "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/assetdefinition/12",
                                type: "fire_pump",
                                display: "Fire Pump"
                            }
                        ]
                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    }

Retrieve detail information for a single task list

    GET /tasklist/123

    200
    {

    }

Retrieve detailed information for a task list which does not exist

    GET /tasklist/987

    404

Create a new task list template not assigned to any asset definitions

    POST /tasklist
    {"type":"template","name":"example template","startsOn":"2023-01-01"}

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "id": 1073904282861633,
            "uri": "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/1073904282861633"
            "name": "example template",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "template",
            "scheduleType": "monthly",
            "parent": null,
            "template": null,
            "version": 1,
            "orderIndex": null,
            "startsOn": "2023-01-01",
            "endsOn": null,
            "asset": null,
            "assetDefinitions": null,
            "statActiveAssets": 0,
            "statActiveAssetsOutdated": 0,
            "statActiveRecurrences": 0
        }
    }

Create a new task list of type template that is assigned to asset definitions

    POST /tasklist
    {"type":"template","name":"example template","startsOn":"2023-01-01", "assetDefinitionIds": [17, 16]}

    200
    {
        "data": {
            "id": 1073904282861633,
            "uri": "http://api.servicetrade.com/api/tasklist/1073904282861633"
            "name": "example template",
            "status": "active",
            "type": "template",
            "scheduleType": "monthly",
            "parent": null,
            "template": null,
            "version": 1,
            "orderIndex": null,
            "startsOn": "2023-01-01",
            "endsOn": null,
            "asset": null,
            "assetDefinitions": [
                {
                    "id": 17,
                    "uri": "http://localhost:8989/api/assetdefinition/17",
                    "type": "__generic",
                    "display": "Generic"
                },
                {
                    "id": 16,
                    "uri": "http://localhost:8989/api/assetdefinition/16",
                    "type": "location",
                    "display": "Location"
                }
            ],
            "statActiveAssets": 0,
            "statActiveAssetsOutdated": 0,
            "statActiveRecurrences": 0
        }
    }

        }

Deleting Task Lists not allowed

    DELETE /tasklist/987

    405

Return to Top

Tax Rate

The tax rate resource is used to retrieve estimated sales tax rates for a location. The stateRate, countyRate, cityRate, and otherRate fields are reserved for future use, and may not be populated.

  • Resource URL: /taxrate/
  • Available Verbs: GET
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /taxrate 200

Query Params

Name Type Value
postalCode string returns sales tax rate information for this postal code
locationId integer returns sales tax rate information for this location

Either postalCode or locationId must be provided, but not both. If postalCode is provided, the tax rates associated with that postal code will be returned. If locationId is provided and the location has an explicitly assigned tax group, that tax group's rates will be returned. Otherwise, the tax rates associated with the location's postal code will be returned.

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
taxGroupCode string sales tax group code.
name string description of the sales tax group
stateRate double This field is reserved for future use, and is not currently populated.
countyRate double This field is reserved for future use, and is not currently populated.
cityRate double This field is reserved for future use, and is not currently populated.
otherRate double This field is reserved for future use, and is not currently populated.
combinedRate double combined sales tax rate as a decimal. (e.g. 8.5% rate is represented as 0.085)

Retrieve tax rates for a given postal code

GET /taxrate?postalCode=90210

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "taxrates": [
            {
                "taxGroupCode": "WAKECO",
                "name": "WAKE COUNTY",
                "stateRate": null,
                "countyRate": null,
                "cityRate": null,
                "otherRate": null,
                "combinedRate": 0.0725
            }
        ]
    }
}

Retrieve tax rates for a given location

GET /taxrate?locationId=123

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "taxrates": [
            {
                "taxGroupCode": "WAKECO",
                "name": "WAKE COUNTY",
                "stateRate": null,
                "countyRate": null,
                "cityRate": null,
                "otherRate": null,
                "combinedRate": 0.0725
            }
        ]
    }
}

No tax rate information found

GET /taxrate?postalCode=99999

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "taxrates": []
    }
}

Return to Top

TaxGroup

The taxgroup resource is used to retrieve lists of tax groups or information about a single tax group.

  • Resource URL: /taxgroup/[taxGroupId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /taxgroup 200
GET /taxgroup/123 200
GET /taxgroup/123 404 tax group 123 not found
POST /taxgroup 200
POST /taxgroup 400 invalid parameters
PUT /taxgroup 405 not allowed
PUT /taxgroup/123 200
PUT /taxgroup/123 400 invalid parameters
PUT /taxgroup/123 404 tax group 123 not found
DELETE /taxgroup 405 not allowed
DELETE /taxgroup/123 204 tax group deleted

Query Params

Name Type Value
code string substring to find in the tax group code
name string substring to find in the tax group name
search string substring to find in the tax group name or code
state string string that exactly equals the state
postalCode string substring to find in the tax group postal codes
excludeTaxGroupId integer exclude the tax group with this id from the results

POST/PUT Parameters

Name Type Required Value
code string on POST Tax Group Code, 40 character max length
name string on POST Tax Group Name, 100 characters max length
combinedRate double on POST Combined tax rate as a decimal (e.g. 8.5% rate is represented as 0.085). Required when combinedRateDetails is empty
combinedRateDetails array (object) no Array of rates. E.g. [{"name": "tax1","taxRate": 0.1},{"name": "tax2","taxRate": 0.2}]
state string on POST State abbreviation
postalCodes array (string) no Array of postal code strings for this group, existing values will be overwritten on PUT

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
code string Tax Group Code
name string Tax Group Name
combinedRate double Combined tax rate as a decimal (e.g. 8.5% rate is represented as 0.085)
combinedRateDetails array (object) Array of tax rates
state string State abbreviation
postalCodes array Array of postal codes this tax group applies to

Retrieve all tax groups

GET /taxgroup

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "taxGroups": [
            {
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5060",
                "id": 5060,
                "code": "LA",
                "name": "Los Angeles",
                "state": "CA",
                "combinedRate": 0.0975,
                "combinedRateDetails": [],
                "postalCodes": [
                    "90024"
                    "90025",
                    "90034",
                    "90035",
                    "90049",
                    "90056",
                ],
                "created": 1619313017,
                "updated": 1619313017
            },
            {
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5087",
                "id": 5087,
                "code": "WAKE2021",
                "name": "Wake County",
                "state": "NC",
                "combinedRate": 0.085,
                "combinedRateDetails": [],
                "postalCodes": [
                    "27513",
                    "27607"
                ],
                "created": 1619314698,
                "updated": 1619314698
            },
            {
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5088",
                "id": 5088,
                "code": "NYC2020",
                "name": "NYC Rates",
                "state": "NY",
                "combinedRate": 0.095,
                "combinedRateDetails": [],
                "postalCodes": [
                    "10001",
                    "10002",
                    "10003",
                    "10004",
                    "10005"
                ],
                "created": 1619315416,
                "updated": 1619315416
            },
            {
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5089",
                "id": 5089,
                "code": "NYC2021",
                "name": "NYC Taxes",
                "state": "NY",
                "combinedRate": 0.0965,
                "combinedRateDetails": [],
                "postalCodes": [
                    "10001",
                    "10002",
                    "10003",
                    "10004",
                    "10005"
                ],
                "created": 1619315416,
                "updated": 1619315416
            }
        ]
    }
}

Retrieve tax groups for a given postal code

GET /taxgroup/?postalCode=10005

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "taxGroups": [
            {
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5088",

                    "10002",

                "state": "NY",

                    "10005"

Modify an existing tax group

PUT /taxgroup/5089
    {"name": "Brooklyn NYC", "combinedRate": 0.0975, "postalCodes": ["11203", "11204", "11205"]}

200
{
    "data": {
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/5089",
        "id": 5089,
        "code": "brooklyn",
        "name": "Brooklyn NYC",
        "state": "NYC",
        "combinedRate": 0.0975,
        "combinedRateDetails": [],
        "postalCodes": [
            "11203",
            "11204",
            "11205"
        ],
        "created": 1619315470,
        "updated": 1619453875
    }
}

Create a new tax group

POST /taxgroup
    {"code": "BOS", "name": "Boston", "state": "MA", "combinedRate": 0.0975, "postalCodes": ["02108", "02109", "02110", "02111", "02113"]}

200
{
    "data": {
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/taxgroup/8166",
        "id": 8166,
        "code": "BOS",
        "name": "Boston",
        "state": "MA",
        "combinedRate": 0.0975,
        "combinedRateDetails": [],
        "postalCodes": [
            "02108",
            "02109",
            "02110",
            "02111",
            "02113"
        ],
        "created": 1619454113,
        "updated": 1619454113
    }
}

Delete a tax group

DELETE /taxgroup/123

204

Return to Top

Terms

The terms resource is used to retrieve lists of terms or detailed information about a single set of terms.

  • Resource URL: /terms/[termsId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /terms 200
GET /terms/123 200
GET /terms/123 404 terms 123 not found
POST /terms 200 terms created
POST /terms 400 bad data for create
POST /terms 403 not allowed
PUT /terms/123 200 terms updated
PUT /terms/123 400 bad data for update
PUT /terms/123 404 terms 123 not found (or not accessible by the user)
PUT /terms/123 403 not allowed
DELETE /terms 403 not allowed
DELETE /terms/123 204 terms deleted
DELETE /terms/123 404 terms 123 not found (or not accessible by the user)
DELETE /terms/123 409 terms is referenced by other entities

Query Params

No query parameters are supported. An index call will return all terms in your account.

POST Parameters

Name Type Required Value
name string yes The title of these terms.
content string yes The content of these terms.

PUT Parameters

Name Type Value
name string The title of these terms.
content string The content of these terms.

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
name string The title of terms.
content string The content of terms.

Retrieve all terms

GET /terms

200
{
    "data": {
        "totalPages": 1,
        "page": 1,
        "terms": [
            {
                "id": 100000000000001,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/terms/1100000000000001",
                "name": "Job Terms",
                "content": "Terative approaches to corporate strategy foster collaborative thinking to further the overall value proposition."
            },
            {
                "id": 100000000000002,
                "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/terms/100000000000002",
                "name": "Quote Terms",
                "content": "Leverage agile frameworks to provide a robust synopsis for high level overviews."
            }
        ]
    }
}

Retrieve one set of terms by id

GET /terms/100000000000002

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 100000000000002,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/terms/100000000000002",
        "name": "Quote Terms",
        "content": "Leverage agile frameworks to provide a robust synopsis for high level overviews."
    }
}

Retrieve information for a set of terms which does not exist

GET /terms/987

404

Create a new set of terms with POST

POST /terms
{"name": "Job Terms 100000000000003", "content": "Don't immanentize the eschalon."}

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 100000000000003,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/terms/100000000000003",
        "name": "Job Terms 100000000000003",
        "content": "Don't immanentize the eschalon."
    }
}

Modify terms with PUT

POST /terms/100000000000003
{"content": "Immanentize the eschalon."}

200
{
    "data": {
        "id": 100000000000003,
        "uri": "https://api.servicetrade.com/api/terms/100000000000003",
        "name": "Job Terms 100000000000003",
        "content": "Immanentize the eschalon."
    }
}

Delete Terms

DELETE /terms/100000000000003

204

Return to Top

Time Card

  • Resource URL: /timecard/[timeCardId]
  • Resource URL: /timecard
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /timecard 200 will return a paginated list of time cards
GET /timecard 400 invalid parameter provided
GET /timecard 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 200 will return requested time card by provided id
GET /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/612666d784681b6b12380000 404 not found
GET /timecard/history 200 will return a paginated list of time card user actions
GET /timecard/history 400 invalid parameter provided
POST /timecard 200
POST /timecard 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard 403 no permission
PUT /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 200
PUT /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 400 incorrect passed params
PUT /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 403 no permission
PUT /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 404 not found
DELETE /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 204
DELETE /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61 403 no permission
DELETE /timecard/612666d784681b6b12380000 404 not found
POST /timecard/generate 200 generate a time card
POST /timecard/generate 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard/generate 403 no permission
POST /timecard/generate 404 not found
POST /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61/share 204 share successful
POST /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61/share 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61/share 403 no permission
POST /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61/share 404 not found
POST /timecard/612666d784681b6b1238db61/share 429 too many requests

Query Params

Name Type Required Value
timeCardId uuid No Comma separated list of time cards ids
userId integer No Comma separated list of user ids
type string No Comma separated list of types, supports labor_item, clock_event, and expense
status string No Comma separated list of statuses, supports unmodified, unsubmitted, submitted, changes_requested, approved, and locked
startDate string or integer No matches records with a start date on the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format, supports comma separated list
startDateBefore string or integer No matches records with a start date on or before the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
startDateAfter string or integer No matches records with a start date on or after the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
endDate string or integer No matches records with a end date on the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format, supports comma separated list
endDateBefore string or integer No matches records with a end date on or before the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
endDateAfter string or integer No matches records with a end date on or after the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
updatedBefore integer No timestamp, matches records updated on or before
updatedAfter integer No timestamp, matches records updated on or after
sort string No field to sort by, one of: type, status, startDate, endDate (created is the default)
sortOrder string No order of sort, either ASC or DESC (ASC is the default)

POST|PUT Parameters

Name Type Required Value
status string For Post available POST options: unmodified, unsubmitted, and submitted. additional PUT options: changes_requested, approved, and locked
type string For Post available options: labor_item, clock_event, expense, not allowed for PUT
userId integer No ID of the user, if not passed will use current user session id, not allowed for PUT
startDate string For Post format YYYY-MM-DD. Must fall on account wise week day config (default is Sunday), not allowed for PUT
endDate string No format YYYY-MM-DD. Must be 7 days out from startDate. For example if startDate is Sunday endDay must fall on Saturday, not allowed for PUT
timeCardId uuid No [Generate only] existing time card id
userIds array For Share [Share only] the array of integer user ids whom the time card will be shared to

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
id uuid id of time card
status string current status of time card
type string type of time card
startDate integer start date for time card
endDate integer end date for time card
user User user assigned to time card
grandTotal float sum of "value" field of associated time card day logs
created integer creation date for time card
updated integer last updated date for time card
submittedOn string deprecated; use submittedTime instead; date when time card status was changed to "submitted" in a format "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss"
submittedTime integer Unix timestamp of the submitted time, the moment when the time card status was changed to "submitted"

GET /timecard/history Query Params

Name Type Required Value
timeCardId uuid No Comma separated list of time cards ids
userAction string No Comma separated list of strings representing actions done on a time card, supports (timecard.approved, timecard.submitted, timecard.change_request)
userActionId integer No Comma separated list of user action ids that will return user action data related to actions done on a time card
userId integer No Comma separated list of user ids that will return user data related to its entity
startDateBefore string No string date format 'YYYY-MM-DD', matches time cards whose start date is before
startDateAfter string No string date format 'YYYY-MM-DD', matches time cards whose start date is after
endDateBefore string No string date format 'YYYY-MM-DD', matches time cards whose end date is before
endDateAfter string No string date format 'YYYY-MM-DD', matches time cards whose end date is after
eventTimeMinute string No string date format 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm', matches time card user actions within minute provided
type string No comma separated list of types, supports labor_item, clock_event, generic, and expense
groupBy string No will group data by specified values, supports userActionId, userAction, eventTimeMinute
sort string No field to sort by, only supports eventTime (eventTime is the default)
sortOrder string No order of sort, either ASC or DESC (ASC is the default)

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
user User resource identifier of the user who performed this action
entity.uri string resource identifier of the entity to which this event is attached
entity.id integer id of the entity to which this event is attached
entity.type entity type entity type constant
properties object/array context specific details about the event, as a JSON object
properties._totalCount integer returned when using groupBy parameter to represent total grouped actions
created integer Unix timestamp of when this event occurred
updated integer Unix timestamp of when this event occurred

Retrieve all time cards

GET /timecard

200
{

        "totalPages": 1,

Time Card Comment

  • Resource URL: /timecard/[timeCardId]/comment/[commentId]
  • Resource URL: /timecard/[timeCardId]/comment
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 200 will return requested time card comment by provided id
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment 200 will return a paginated list of time card comments
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment 400 invalid parameter provided
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 404 not found
POST /timecard/1238db61/comment 200
POST /timecard/1238db61/comment 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard/1238db61/comment 403 no permission
POST /timecard/1238db61/comment 404 not found
PUT /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 200
PUT /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 400 incorrect passed params
PUT /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 403 no permission
PUT /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 404 not found
DELETE /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 204
DELETE /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 403 no permission
DELETE /timecard/1238db61/comment/34b234ba 404 not found

Query Params

Name Type Required Value
commentId string No comma seperated time card comment ids
authorId string No comma seperated author (user) ids
visibility string No possible values: public, private

POST|PUT Parameters

Name Type Required Value
content string For Post text of comment
visibility array For Post array of visibilities. For now allowed options: 'public' or 'private'
authorId integer For Post ID of the user

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
id uuid id of time card comment
uri string link to time card comment
content string message itself
created integer creation date for time card
updated integer/null last updated date for time card
visibility array possible values are: public, private
entity array entity to which this comment is attached
author User/null author of the comment

Time Card Day Log

Resource URL: /timecard/daylog/[id] Resource URL: /timecard/daylog Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 200 will return requested day log by provided id
GET /timecard/daylog 200 will return a paginated list of day logs
GET /timecard/daylog 400 invalid parameter provided
GET /timecard/daylog 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 401 not logged in
GET /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b12380000 404 not found
POST /timecard/daylog 200
POST /timecard/daylog 200
POST /timecard/daylog 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard/daylog 403 no permission
POST /timecard/daylogs 200 create multiple day logs
POST /timecard/daylogs 400 incorrect passed params
POST /timecard/daylogs 403 no permission
PUT /timecard/daylogs 200 update multiple day logs
PUT /timecard/daylogs 400 incorrect passed params
PUT /timecard/daylogs 403 no permission
PUT /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 200
PUT /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 404 not found
PUT /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 403 no permission
DELETE /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 204
DELETE /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61 403 no permission
DELETE /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b12380000 404 not found

Query Params

Name Type Required Value
dayLogId uuid No comma separated list of day log ids
timeCardId uuid No comma separated list of time card ids
userId integer No comma separated list of user ids
type string No comma separated list of types, supports labor_item, clock_event, generic, and expense
timeCardType string No comma separated list of time card types, supports labor_item, clock_event, and expense
status string No comma separated list of time card status types, supports changes_requested, approved, submitted, and unsubmitted
loggedDate integer or string No matches records with a logged date on the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format, supports comma separated list
loggedDateBefore integer or string No matches records with a logged date before the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
loggedDateAfter integer or string No matches records with a logged date after the specified timestamp or date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
value double No comma separated list of values
labelId integer No comma separated list of group label ids
clockEventId integer No comma separated list of clock event ids
jobItemId integer No comma separated list of job item ids
updatedBefore integer No timestamp, matches records updated on or before
updatedAfter integer No timestamp, matches records updated on or after
sort string No field to sort by, one of: type, loggedDate (created is the default)
sortOrder string No order of sort, either ASC or DESC (ASC is the default)

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
id uuid id of day log
timecard Time Card time card associated with day log
type string type of day log
clockEvents array list of clock events, will only be a pair of clock events
jobItem Job Item job item associated with day log
loggedDate string logged date in YYYY-MM-DD format
label Group Label group label attached to generic day log
value string value for day log, can represent quantity, hours, or expenses
created integer creation date for time card
updated integer last updated date for time card

Note: When creating or updating the loggedDate or value of a day log with type labor_item it will also update the quantity (value) and/or usedOn (loggedDate) of associated job item

POST Params

Name Type For type "generic" For type "clock_event" For type "labor_item" For type "expense" Value
timeCardId uuid required required required required id of time card
type string required required required required type of day log
loggedDate string required invalid optional invalid logged date, format 'YYYY-MM-DD'
value float required invalid optional invalid value for day log, only required for generic type. All other types will generate their own values
labelId integer required ignored ignored ignored label for generic type
clockEventId array ignored required ignored ignored array of clock event ids
jobItemId integer ignored ignored optional required job item id, when not provided for labor_item you can provide a jobId, libItemId, and value to create a new job item
jobId integer ignored ignored optional ignored job id, required with type labor_item when a jobItemId isn't provided
libItemId integer ignored ignored optional ignored lib item id, required with type labor_item when a jobItemId isn't provided
serviceLineId integer ignored ignored optional ignored service line id, required with type labor_item when a jobItemId isn't provided and lib item id provided doesn't have a service line

PUT Params

Name Type For type "generic" For type "clock_event" For type "labor_item" For type "expense" Value
timeCardId uuid optional optional optional optional id of time card
type string optional optional optional optional type of day log
loggedDate string optional invalid optional invalid logged date, format 'YYYY-MM-DD'
value float optional invalid optional invalid value for day log, only required for generic type. All other types will generate their own values
labelId integer optional optional optional ignored label for generic type
clockEventId array ignored optional ignored ignored array of clock event ids
jobItemId integer ignored ignored optional optional job item id

                    "email": "joey.shmoey@gsd.com",

                },

Delete a day log

DELETE /timecard/daylog/612666d784681b6b1238db61

200

Return to Top

Token

The token resource is used to generate tokens that grant specific users limited access to specific resources.

The token must then be added to the URL of the resource. For example, a Service Link URL would be: https://app.servicetrade.com/customer/jobsummary?id=<TOKEN>

The URL for viewing a Quote would be: https://app.servicetrade.com/quoteview/view?id=<TOKEN>

The URL for viewing an Invoice would be: https://app.servicetrade.com/customer/invoice?id=<TOKEN>

  • Resource URL: /token
  • Available Verbs: GET
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /token/ 200
GET /token/ 400 invalid request parameters
GET /token/ 404 requested resource not found

Query Params

Name Type Value
contactId integer id of a contact to grant access to (supplied contact must have an email address)
userId integer id of a user to grant access to
jobId integer id of the job to grant access to
quoteId integer id of the quote to grant access to
invoiceId integer id of the invoice to grant access to
changeOrderId integer id of the change order to grant access to

The only combinations of query parameters that are valid are:

contactId & jobId
userId & jobId
contactId & quoteId
userId & quoteId
contactId & invoiceId
userId & invoiceId
contactId & changeOrderId
userId & changeOrderId

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
token string value to be included in a request to a protected resource

Generate a token for a contact to view a Job's Service Link

GET /token?jobId=123&contactId=456

200
{
    "data": {
        "token": "ol%2FR5B63qOAyvSdX1dBFb%2BObCYgKI%2FqznCdrJd9t57zoKTxqTv6Nuaa1L2Ey54Og23m06hAGUL7q8GQ%3D"
    }
}

Generate a token for a contact to view a Quote

GET /token?quoteId=123&contactId=456

200
{
    "data": {
        "token": "loCCqViaAlPrYnI87RnnYDuKVXup2n%2FA1l%2BdTu2sDuuXsbz4b7osprjVe8VHv7l%2Bib8qGzmWZOM%3D"
    }
}

Generate a token for a contact to view an Invoice

GET /token?invoiceId=1&contactId=84

200
{
    "data": {
        "token": "bpplYCSa6efDb6BftKPT95rvCs0Dd2gjPP2whs8OOw76JtDagmAPFU4Hc%2BjEYe3FBrvveAyZIWQduDRW"
    }
}

Return to Top

User

The user resource is used to retrieve lists of users or detailed information about a single user. Only returns active users.

  • Resource URL: /user/[userId]
  • Available Verbs: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • Authentication Required: yes

Response Codes

Verb Example URL Code Reason
GET /user 200
GET /user/123 200
GET /user/123 404 user 123 not found
GET /user/123/role 200 get roles assigned to user
GET /user/123/role 404 user 123 not found
POST /user 403 no permission
POST /user 200
POST /user/123/role/456 200 assign role 456 to user 123
POST /user/123/role/456 400 invalid role
POST /user/123/role/456 403 no permission
POST /user/123/role/456 404 user 123 not found
PUT /user/123 403 no permission
PUT /user/123 404 not found
PUT /user/123 200
DELETE /user 405 not allowed
DELETE /user/123/role/456 200 unassign role 456 from user 123
DELETE /user/123/role/456 400 invalid role
DELETE /user/123/role/456 403 no permission
DELETE /user/123/role/456 404 user 123 not found

Query Params

Name Type Value
name string (deprecated) use "search" param instead
companyId integer returns only users who belong to the given company (will default to the logged-in user's company if not supplied)
locationId integer returns only users who belong to the given location
isTech boolean (see note below) returns only users who are techs (if true/1 provided) or aren't techs (if false/0/[blank] provided)
isHelper boolean (see note below) returns only users who are helpers (if true/1 provided) or aren't helpers (if false/0/[blank] provided)
isTechOrHelper boolean (see note below) returns only users who are either techs or helpers (if true/1 provided) or are neither techs nor helpers (if false/0/[blank] provided)
isSales boolean returns only users who are salespeople (if true/1 provided) or aren't salespeople (if false/0 provided)
excludeUserIds array excludes these users from the potential matches
status string comma-separated list of statuses: "active", "pending", "inactive" (will default to "active" if not supplied)
createdBefore integer timestamp, matches records created on or before
createdAfter integer timestamp, matches records created on or after
updatedBefore integer timestamp, matches records updated on or before
updatedAfter integer timestamp, matches records updated on or after
search string returns only users whose name, username, email, company name or external id includes this string (case insensitive). the actual fields list is defined by "searchFields" param
searchFields string a comma-separated list of the following values: "name", "username", "email", "companyName", "externalId". this param limits the search scope for the "search" param. empty value means "all fields"
Note:

Only one of isTech/isHelper/isTechOrHelper parameters is allowed in a request. If there is any combination of these parameters, error 400 is returned.

POST/PUT Parameters

Name Type Required for POST Value
username string Yes username for user, must be unique; in PUT cannot be changed simultaneously with email
firstName string Yes first name of user
lastName string Yes last name of user
password string Yes password for user. must be 12 or more characters long and include at least one letter and at least one number
email string Yes email for user; in PUT cannot be changed simultaneously with username
phone string No phone number for user
companyId integer Yes user's company
locationId integer Yes user's home office
details string No brief information about this user, visible to the public.
status string No user's status, one of: active, inactive, pending, shadow. defaults to active if not provided.
isSales boolean No is the user a salesperson? defaults to no.
timezone string No user timezone identifier; if not provided, the account's time zone is used instead
serviceLineIds array (ServiceLine) No the service lines a user is capable of providing service for. defaults to none.
managerId integer No id of the user assigned as the manager of this user
mfaRequired boolean No is multi-factor authentication required for this user?

The user status "shadow" is reserved for system users and should not be used for users who need user interface or API access.

Returned Elements

Name Type Value
users array (User) list of user objects
name string user full name
status string user status
email string user email address
avatar array (objects) user avatar URIs
firstName string user first name
lastName string user last name
username string user login name
phone string user phone number
timezone string user timezone identifier
isTech boolean is this user a technician?
isSales boolean is this user a sales representative?
isHelper boolean is this user a helper?
company Company company to which this user belongs
location Location location of this user's primary office
account Account account to which this user belongs
details string brief information about this user, visible to the public
activities array list of permission activities this user has (note that some activities are only visible if the associated feature is enabled in the account)
serviceLines array (Service Line) list of service lines provided by this user
roles array (Role) list of roles applied to this user
externalIds object a key/value object of external system ids, only returned if account has external integrations defined
created integer Unix timestamp of this record's creation time
updated integer Unix timestamp of when this record was last updated
manager User user who has been assigned as the manager of this user
mfaRequired boolean is multi-factor authentication required for this user?

Retrieve all users that match a given name

GET /user?name=art

200
